data.description:
# P1.2 — rebar export / import (JSON snapshot + GitHub-issues ingest)

## Context (problem + research)

rebar has no export/import; the git history is the only "export". git-issue
(dspinellis) imports & exports GitHub/GitLab issues; git-bug bridges them. Export
enables migration, backup, CI snapshots; import enables onboarding existing
trackers. Additive and low-risk: import is just composed `create` events through
the normal locked write path (never raw-event injection, so I2/I5 hold), and is
purely additive to a non-empty store (never updates/deletes).

## Evidence (experiments)

- Export reuses the existing `ticket_state` output schema (already validated across
  interfaces); only an envelope schema is new.
- Round-trip property: export -> fresh repo -> import -> export yields the same
  logical state (modulo new ids/timestamps).

## What / Implementation detail

1. Read command `export`: `reduce_all_tickets` ->
   `{schema_version, exported_at, tickets:[<ticket_state>...]}`; per-ticket shape is
   the existing schema; add `schemas/export.schema.json` envelope AND an MCP
   `outputSchema` registration (parity with other read tools).
2. Write command `import`: per record compose `CREATE` (+ follow-on
   `COMMENT`/`LINK`/`tag`) via `_commands/composer.py` through `event_append`.
   `--id-map` records source->new-id so links resolve. Adapters in new
   `src/rebar/_io/`: `export_json.py`, `import_json.py`, `import_github.py`
   (maps GH issue fields -> create args; network READ only, no write to GitHub).
3. MCP: `export_tickets` (read), `import_tickets` (write, gated by
   `mcp_server.py:_readonly()`). Help texts for both.

## Success Criteria

- `rebar export -o json` emits a schema-valid envelope; importing it into a fresh
  repo reproduces the same logical state.
- GitHub import maps a recorded fixture issue (+labels/comments) to a rebar ticket.
- import writes only existing event types (no SCHEMA_VERSION bump); never mutates
  existing tickets.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `export` command + `schemas/export.schema.json` + MCP outputSchema
- [ ] `import` command (composed creates through the locked path; `--id-map` for links)
- [ ] `src/rebar/_io/` adapters: export_json, import_json, import_github
- [ ] round-trip test; GitHub adapter test against a recorded fixture (`integration` marker for live)
- [ ] MCP `import_tickets` write-gated by `_readonly()`

See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.2).
data.title: P1.2 — rebar export / import (JSON snapshot + GitHub-issues ingest)
